commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=fd7959904a8b202fad34d488c584302bb11063c3)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a streaming reasoning-tag filter and wires it into the active and legacy OpenAI-compatible stream parsers, with unit and parser regression tests for mirrored `<think>` and `reasoning_content` chunks.

PR surface: Source +139, Tests +198. Total +337 across 6 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main appends streamed `delta.content` to visible text and separately emits `reasoning_content` as thinking, but no live MiniMax/channel run was available in the PR proof.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor real behavior proof and a maintainer-visible code fix for the literal-tag regression; ClawSweeper should not queue a repair while the external proof gate remains mock-only.

target.title: fix(agents): keep inline <think> reasoning out of OpenAI-compatible visible text
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #88741.

When a model streams inline `<think>…</think>` reasoning in `delta.content` over the OpenAI-compatible streaming path (e.g. `minimax/MiniMax-M2.7`), the parsers routed the raw `delta.content` string straight into a **visible text** content part. Combined with the provider mirroring the same reasoning into `reasoning_content` (which becomes a `thinking` part), the assistant message ended up with the chain-of-thought in *both* the text part and the thinking part — and channel renderers (Telegram, Discord) show the text part, leaking the CoT verbatim before the answer. The leak is also persisted into the session content parts.

This adds a stateful streaming reasoning-tag text filter (`createReasoningTagTextFilter`) — a direct sibling of the existing `createDeepSeekTextFilter` — that strips inline `<think>/<thinking>/<thought>/antthinking` spans from the **visible text** stream at the parser boundary, using the exact same tag vocabulary as the already-shipped delivery-time `stripReasoningTagsFromText` sanitizer. It is wired into both OpenAI-compatible parsers:

- `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.ts` (active transport-stream path) — chained after the DSML filter in the visible-text path.
- `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.ts` (legacy `openai-completions` provider path) — content routed through the filter before being appended to the text block.

Reasoning still reaches the thinking channel via the existing `reasoning_content` / structured-thinking paths, so the reported case (reasoning mirrored into both `delta.content` and `reasoning_content`) now produces clean visible text **and a single thinking part — with no new duplication into thinking**. An unclosed-`<think>` recovery flush ensures a malformed stream never drops the answer. This is defense-in-front-of the delivery-time sanitizer, which remains as defense-in-depth.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed:** Inline `<think>…</think>` reasoning streamed in `delta.content` (with mirrored `reasoning_content`) no longer leaks into the visible text content part on either OpenAI-compatible parser; reasoning is only present in the thinking part.
- **Real environment tested:** Local Linux source checkout (Node v22.19.0), repo vitest via `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs`. No live MiniMax key was available, so this is source-level reproduction (the same basis as the ClawSweeper source-repro on this issue).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts`
- **Evidence after fix:**

  Before the fix (parser/filter changes stashed, repro test kept), the active-parser suite fails with the CoT leaked into the visible text part:

  ```
  FAIL src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts
    > keeps inline <think> reasoning out of visible text parts on the completions stream
  Expected: "Here are three great thrillers."
  Received: "<think>User wants thrillers. Recommend three.</think>Here are three great thrillers."
  ```

  After the fix, all three acceptance-criteria suites pass:

  ```
  src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts        Test Files 1 passed   Tests 68 passed
  src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts  Test Files 1 passed   Tests  7 passed
  src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts    Test Files 1 passed   Tests 214 passed
  ```

  `pnpm tsgo:core` (prod typecheck), `oxfmt --check`, and `oxlint` on the changed files all pass.
- **Observed result after fix:** The visible text part equals only the answer (`"Here are three great thrillers."`), contains neither `<think>` nor the reasoning text, and the reasoning appears only in the thinking part (`"User wants thrillers. Recommend three."`).
- **What was not tested:** A live `minimax/MiniMax-M2.7` run over a real Telegram/Discord channel (no provider key in this environment).

## Verification

- New unit tests for the streaming filter: `src/shared/text/reasoning-tags.test.ts`.
- New parser integration tests: `src/agents/openai-transport-stream.test.ts`, `src/llm/providers/openai-completions.test.ts`.
- Acceptance-criteria suites from the issue review run green (see Evidence).
